# How to read these docs

Every contract page follows the same structure, generated from the deployed
contract's ABI and merged with hand-written behavior notes. Because the
mechanical parts are derived rather than written, a signature in these docs is
the signature on chain.

## Contract pages

Each page opens with the mainnet address (linked to
[evm.now](https://evm.now), a multi-chain explorer) and the deploy block, then:

- **Concepts** ŌĆö the mental model for the contract, when it needs one
- **Write functions** ŌĆö every state-changing function, main entry points
  first. Each shows the Solidity signature, an **Access** line, behavior
  prose, and revert conditions
- **Read functions** ŌĆö every view function, alphabetical
- **Events** ŌĆö every event with its indexed fields and when it fires
- **Errors** ŌĆö every custom error and the condition that raises it

## Access lines

Every write function declares who can call it:

- **permissionless** ŌĆö anyone. Several of these pay the caller a small
  reward; see [Run a keeper](/docs/guides/run-a-keeper)
- **\<contract\>-only** ŌĆö callable only by another protocol contract
  (for example patron-only, module-only, hook-only)
- **admin-gated** ŌĆö gated by [ProtocolAdmin](/docs/contracts/protocol-admin),
  which auto-locks 1 year after deploy. Four scoped carve-outs survive the
  lock; each is labeled where it appears
- **deployer one-shot** ŌĆö a setup function that was called once during the
  deploy broadcast and is permanently closed

The full function-by-function table is at
[Access control](/docs/reference/access-control).

## Examples

Read examples use `cast` (Foundry) against a free public RPC:

```bash
cast call 0xŌĆ” "bidBalance()(uint256)" --rpc-url https://ethereum-rpc.publicnode.com
```

Write examples use [viem](https://viem.sh). ABIs for every contract are
served at `/abis/<ContractName>.json` and live in the repo at
`app/lib/abis/`.

## Units and terms

- Amounts are wei unless stated otherwise; `bps` means basis points on the
  denominator stated at the use site (most contracts use 10,000; the hook's
  volume caps use 100,000)
- Punk ids are `uint16` (0..9999); trait ids are `uint8` (0..110)
- The ERC20 is written **$111** in prose. Its on-chain symbol is `111`
- CryptoPunks themselves are not ERC721 tokens. They live in the 2017
  CryptoPunksMarket contract and move via `transferPunk`, `buyPunk`, and
  `offerPunkForSaleToAddress`; sale proceeds queue in `pendingWithdrawals`
  and are collected with `withdraw()`

## Machine-readable surfaces

| Surface | Path |
| --- | --- |
| Protocol manifest (addresses + ABIs + docs links) | [`/protocol-manifest.json`](/protocol-manifest.json) |
| Per-contract ABI | `/abis/<ContractName>.json` |
| LLM orientation | [`/llms.txt`](/llms.txt) |
| Search corpus for this reference | `/docs-search-index.json` |

The markdown source of every page is in the public repo under
`docs/reference/`, regenerated by `scripts/generate-docs.ts`.
